title: Life with arthritis in Canada: a personal and public health challenge - Canada.ca
content:
## Life with arthritis in Canada: a personal and public health challenge
For readers interested in the full version of this report, the document is available for downloading or viewing:
Life with Arthritis in Canada : A personal and public health challenge (PDF Document - 2.92 MB - 128 pages)
Life with Arthritis in Canada : A personal and public health challenge
- A few words from the Chief Public Health Officer
- Chapter 1 / What is arthritis and how common is it?
- Chapter 2 / Prevention and management
- Chapter 3 / Disability and quality of life
- Chapter 4 / Arthritis among First Nations, Métis and Inuit
- Chapter 5 / Mortality burden of arthritis
- Chapter 6 / Economic burden of arthritis
- Chapter 7 / Arthritis-related prescription medication use
- Chapter 8 / Ambulatory care services utilization
- Chapter 9 / Hospital services for arthritis
To obtain hard copies, please contact:
Chronic Disease Surveillance and Monitoring Division Centre for Chronic Disease Prevention and Control 785 Carling Avenue, AL: 6806B Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0K9 Canada E-mail: chronic.publications.chronique@phac-aspc.gc.ca
